brief_justification: Paper A constructs a family of regular axisymmetric black-hole spacetimes in general relativity, parameterized by mass and spin and interpolating to the Kerr solution; Paper B computes the exact excess L^2(P) risk of a non-Gaussian basis mismatch in Wiener-Hermite system identification via Hankel-Cholesky factorizations and a diagonal solve. There is no shared governing process or structure — a spacetime metric geometry versus a weighted-L^2 moment-based estimation penalty — so no coherent role-for-role mapping holds.
